Meta.title: Leandro Gómez Cano — one startup per month
Meta.description: Regional Director of Operations at tizo. I ship one product idea per month until year-end and document the process in public: startups, operations, growth, AI, and execution.
Meta.ogDescription: One new product each month until December. Public documentation of process, decisions, and metrics. Operations, growth, and applied AI.
Meta.twitterDescription: Build in public: monthly launches, learnings, and outcomes. Startup operator and product builder.

About.title: Operations and product on the same plane
About.name: Leandro Gómez Cano
About.body: I'm Leandro Gómez Cano. I have six years of experience in startups. I'm Regional Director of Operations at tizo and former Country Manager at hugo app. I work with startups and high-tempo teams on operations, growth, artificial intelligence, and product building.
About.purpose: My purpose is to strengthen Nicaragua's startup ecosystem and help founders ship their ideas.

Challenge.title: Building in public, every month of the year

blocks.label: What it is
blocks.text: A commitment through December: each month a new product idea—from problem definition to launch and a minimum viable iteration.

blocks.label: Why
blocks.text: Speed forces decisions. Documenting in the open enforces clarity, cuts self-deception, and accelerates learning against real markets.

blocks.label: What I'll build
blocks.text: Actionable prototypes and MVPs: tools, flows, and experiments that can be validated with users without empty narrative.

blocks.label: What I share
blocks.text: Scope decisions, trade-offs, metrics that matter, mistakes, and adjustments. No glamour: judgment and numbers when relevant.

blocks.label: What I want to learn
blocks.text: How to compress validation time, what to repeat in operations, and how to fold AI into the product cycle without losing business focus.

Classes.title: Hands-on training, going from idea to a tangible product.
Classes.subtitle: Sessions focused on tools and operational judgment. Workshops, 1:1 mentoring and special guests.

Classes.formMailSubject: First cohort priority — AI chatbot class

items.title: AI chatbot for entrepreneurs
items.description: How to design and ship an AI chatbot aligned with your offer, data, and operations—not a generic demo. For founders and teams who need conversion and clarity, not just technology.
items.schedule: Starts May 19, 2026 · Tuesdays and Thursdays at 6:30 pm · online

syllabus.label: Week 1
syllabus.title: MVP definition, ICP, and smoke tests

syllabus.label: Week 3
syllabus.title: Introduction to GitHub, Vercel, and Supabase

syllabus.label: Week 4
syllabus.title: Build your first chatbot with n8n on Telegram and WhatsApp

syllabus.label: Week 5
syllabus.title: Build your first chatbot with Cursor

Roadmap.title: One launch per month · 2026
Roadmap.subtitle:
Remaining months of the year. Status and learnings update each cycle.
We will be building in public, going from idea to product in one month, every month of the year.

items.description: A product for pet adoption: connect shelters and adopters with clear criteria, useful filters, and an application flow without unnecessary friction.
items.keyLearning: First cycle in progress: validate pet supply and adoption criteria before scaling features.

detailSections.title: Problem and scope
detailSections.body: Conversations with shelters and adopters to narrow the flow: which data matters first, where trust breaks down, and what we can validate with a low-friction MVP.

detailSections.title: What we're building
detailSections.body: Screen structure, first application flow, and filtering criteria to prioritize matches between supply and demand.

detailSections.title: Decisions
detailSections.body: Prioritize adoption criteria before adding secondary features; measure a full first application before scaling.

ForWho.title: You don't need to know how to code

items.title: Founders & entrepreneurs
items.body: Who want to ship AI-powered products without relying on a full technical team from day one.

items.title: Operations teams
items.body: Looking to automate processes, improve customer service, and make decisions with real data.

items.title: Operators in LATAM
items.body: Working in markets where execution and speed matter.

items.quote: The course gave me clarity on how to integrate AI into my business without complicating operations.
items.name: María L.

items.quote: I thought I needed a big technical team. In 6 weeks I launched my first chatbot.
items.name: Carlos R.

items.quote: The methodology is practical and direct. No filler—pure judgment and execution.
items.name: Ana S.

items.q: Do I need to know how to code to take the classes?
items.a: No. Classes are designed for founders and teams without a technical background. We use no-code and low-code tools.

items.q: What's the difference between the classes and the monthly challenge?
items.a: Classes are structured training in 6-week cohorts. The challenge is a build-in-public exercise where I ship a new product every month.
$150 USD for the full 6-week cohort. You can pay in cash or by bank transfer.
Fill out the priority form in the Classes section. We'll follow up with next steps and payment options.

items.q: Are classes online or in person?
items.a: Online, live via video call. Tuesdays and Thursdays at 6:30 PM.

Thesis.title: Beliefs I bet on
lines: AI changes how products are designed and shipped.
Latin America needs more operators who turn ideas into systems.
What you don't measure gets mistaken for progress.
Clarity is a competitive asset—you train it with explicit decisions.

FinalCta.title: Stop planning. Start building.
FinalCta.body: Join the classes, follow the monthly challenge, or just watch how it's done in public. Pick your path.

Site.headline: Learn with AI. Ship real projects.
